title: zehneria capillacea
intro: Zehneria capillacea, also known as the Indian pennywort or Makoi, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Zehneria capillacea include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic, as well as exhibiting anti-aging and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Zehneria capillacea can be consumed as a decoction, made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a powder added to food or beverages.
